mappings.rationale: Directly covers endocrine systems in animals including vertebrate and invertebrate hormonal coordination, and the relationship between endocrine and neural regulation.
Addresses nervous systems in animals, including centralized nervous systems, the brain and its components, and neural processing of sensory information.
Covers the nervous system's role in information reception, processing, and integration, central to the coordination of vital processes.
Addresses homeostasis, endocrine regulation, and the nervous system's role in maintaining steady states in the human body.
Directly relevant to sensory reception, specifically photoreceptors and the evolution of specialized sensory organs.
Directly covers the biological clock and periodicity including solar-day rhythms, the mechanism of the biological clock, and factors affecting biological periodicities.
Addresses higher-level information processing in nervous systems and the integration of sensory information.

mappings.vsiAuthor: Steven W. Lockley, Russell G. Foster
mappings.rationale: Covers biological rhythms, dormancy states, and the neural regulation of metabolic rate, directly relevant to biological periodicity and homeostasis.
Addresses chemoreception as a form of sensory reception, including the evolution of chemosensory systems.
Covers sensory reception mechanisms, particularly nociception, and the neural processing of sensory information.
Addresses sensory information processing and the interactions between sense cells and sensory neurons.
Illustrates the neural basis of sensory reception and how cross-modal sensory processing reveals the organization of sensory systems.
